mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on future cash flows and eases financial conditions, so sustained cuts predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is falsified if rate cuts are not followed by positive index performance.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS has declined over the prior 3 months, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 in a rolling 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals labor market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and slower demand, which should pressure equities lower over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment is accelerating rather than merely elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in retail sales and housing starts together indicates broad domestic demand acceleration, which supports near-term revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is not valid if only one demand proxy improves while the other weakens.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS rising and HOUST rising does not outperform either proxy alone, or if the 60-day forward return after joint strength is not positive with statistically meaningful excess over baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL reflects real activity gains without inflation pressure, which improves profit expectations and supports positive equity returns over the next month. The mechanism fails when output growth is inflationary.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and flat-to-lower CPIAUCSL do not show higher 20-day SPX returns than periods with rising INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level relative to the recent environment tightens valuation multiples and raises recession risk, so the market should underperform over the next 20 trading days when policy remains restrictive. The signal is strongest after sustained hikes.
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not negative in a majority of periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, or if the average return in that regime is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth captures the marginal improvement in labor income and supports consumer spending, so rising PAYEMS should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next week when the labor market is not already deteriorating. The signal weakens if payroll gains are purely noise around a flat trend.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises for 2 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: Reject if two-month streaks of PAYEMS gains are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average 5-day return after the trigger is not above the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDPC1 with stronger RSAFS indicates synchronized real and nominal demand growth, which should lift earnings expectations and equity prices over the next quarter. The effect is falsified when GDP growth is accompanied by weak sales growth.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns more often than standalone GDP growth periods, or if the joint effect is not larger than either marginal effect.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates fall and the market should reprice toward lower real yields, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal disappears when inflation is stable or policy is already tight.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month over month while FEDFUNDS < 3%
regime_hint: early inflation shock with easy policy
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration episodes under low FEDFUNDS do not generate negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the interaction is not more negative than either proxy alone.
